sourceMovie.title: Metropolis
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Metropolis works as a animation movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The animation and science fiction blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Ghost in the Shell
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Metropolis clicked for you, Ghost in the Shell (1995) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and science fiction, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Metropolis?
faq.answer: Try Ghost in the Shell, Astro Boy, Evangelion: 1.0 You Are (Not) Alone—each shares DNA with Metropolis while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Metropolis?
faq.answer: Metropolis (2001) is best described as Animation, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Metropolis worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
